Rebrand from PoolGuard to AquaAssure completed 2026-04-27. All user-visible text, titles, manifest, service worker, email templates, and documentation updated. LFC crest/logo left in place as client branding.

**Why:** Danny requested full rebrand — AquaAssure is the product name, PoolGuard was the working title.

**How to apply:** Use "AquaAssur

AquaAssure is being deployed at Liverpool FC's AXA Training Ground on a dedicated tablet.

**Active branch**: `commercial-week1-foundation`

**Immediate priorities** (from CLAUDE.md + onboarding):

2. Remove turbidity parameter (not needed at this facility)
3. Rebrand PoolGuard -> AquaAss

name: Deployment and infrastructure references
description: How to deploy AquaAssure and key Supabase/Vercel details
type: reference
---

- **Vercel deploy**: `npx vercel --prod` (GitHub webhook unreliable, manual deploy preferred)
- **Supabase project**: https://hkfzzviwialzuqrlpbes.supabase.co
- **Live URL**: poolguard-beta.vercel.app (to be moved to lfc.aquaassure.co.uk)
- **Domain**: aquaassure.co.uk
- **Supabase storage bucket**: microbiological-reports (private, RLS enforced)

name: Danny - AquaAssure founder

The LWS site contact form (`app/components/CTA.tsx`) POSTs to `app/api/contact/route.ts`, which sends via Resend.

Working production config (Vercel env, project `local-web-studio-redesign-1`):
- `RESEND_FROM` = `Local Web Studio <danny@send.localwebstudio

name: lws-offer-repositioning
description: LWS no longer promises a free demo to every visitor; the offer is a tailored homepage direction for suitable projects only (July 2026).
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: cf60aa88-4a67-46a0-a6b0-75243313c5a7
---

As of 10 July 2026, Local Web Studio repositioned its sales offer site-wide. Never write copy promising "a free demo", "a working demo before payment", "you keep the demo" or "no obligation" design work.

**Why:**

name: vercel-framework-null-404
description: "Vercel 404 at root despite a passing build — caused by framework preset being null/\"Other\" on an App Router app"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: project
  originSessionId: 2aec8736-5ce4-4d58-98ee-d3d96899f16e
---

When a Next.js App Router site builds successfully on Vercel but returns 404 at the root path `/`, check the Vercel project's Framework Preset. If `get_project` shows `"framework": null` (i.e. preset is "Other"), Vercel runs `next
File unknown was edited 60 times this session — likely a hot path worth monitoring.
